content.text: "All equipment listed herein is available through licensed distributors, approved secondhand vendors, and whichever back-alley contact you trust most. Quality and legality may vary."
Every weapon sits on a triangle: Flesh Damage, Anti-Armor, and Price. You only get two.
• Damage + Cheap: Great vs flesh, useless vs armor, affordable. • Damage + Anti-Armor: Effective vs both, costs a fortune. • Anti-Armor + Cheap: Cracks armor for the team, won't kill alone.

rows: Blunt force through plate. Mauls, power fists.
Finds gaps. Stilettos, AP rounds. Delivers poison.
Reduces armor Value by X per die
AP munitions. Pen 3 vs Armor 6 = treats as Armor 3.
Permanently reduces armor Value by X per hit
Weakens armor for the whole squad. Force multiplier.
Guaranteed X damage to Armor HP per hit, ignores all defenses
Inevitable chip. Gauntlets denting plate every punch.
Bonus X damage to Armor HP per hit

content.text: Melee weapons use Chrome for accuracy. Weapons with the Finesse tag use Reflex instead — speed and precision over brute force. They're cheap to maintain, never need recharging, and most contractors carry at least a knife as backup.

rows: Pen 1. Stagger on crit (-1 AP next turn)
Reach. +2 acc at Close, -2 at Adjacent
Cleave: on crit, one adjacent enemy takes half damage
Pen 1. Disarm on crit (target drops weapon)
Reach. +2 acc at Close, -2 at Adjacent. Sweep on crit (one Close enemy takes half damage)
+2 acc if moved toward target. Bleed 2 on crit
+2 dmg/die vs Armor 3+. Shatter on crit (target -1 Armor, stacks)
Reach. Pen 1. +2 acc at Close, -2 at Adjacent. Bleed 1 on crit
Reach. +2 acc at Close, -2 at Adjacent. Set: +2 acc vs targets who moved toward you
Caves in armor. Stuns on crit (-2 AP next turn)
Peels armor for the whole squad

content.text: These are team weapons. The gauntlet user isn't the killer — they're the can opener. Break the shell, drop the tool, draw your real weapon.

rows: 1 armor HP/hit guaranteed. 5 atks/turn. Can opener.
Bypasses absorb. Finds gaps. Delivers poison.

content.text: Ranged weapons use Reflex for accuracy. They require Charges — an ongoing cost that makes ammo management part of the economic game.

rows: Suppress on crit (target -2 acc next turn)
Must retrieve after combat. Stagger on crit (-1 AP next turn)
No range penalties. Pin on crit (target -1 Move next turn)
+2 acc Adjacent/Close. -2 Medium+. Knockback 1 range band on hit at Adjacent
+2 acc Medium+. -2 Adjacent. Suppress on crit (target -2 acc next turn)
+2 acc if didn't move. Pen 1. Stagger on crit (-1 AP next turn)
+2 acc Adjacent. -2 Medium+. Knockback 1 range band on hit at Adjacent
Pen 2. +2 acc if stationary. +2 acc if target stationary. Stagger on crit (-1 AP next turn)
Expensive ammo pierces plate. +2 acc if stationary.

content.text: Rift patterns and tech weapons use Interface for accuracy. Rift weapons have Penetration 1 baseline and enhanced damage dice to compensate for their toxicity cost.

rows: Heals 50% of damage dealt. +2 Tox/enc. Pen 1
Chains to 1 adjacent (half dmg, no acc roll)
Melee. Push 1 range band on crit
Channel Rift Patterns (+1 check). +1 Tox/enc. Pen 1

content.text: How you compose your squad's loadout determines how you crack armored targets. No single weapon does everything — the triangle forces team coordination.

Loadout: 2× Corp Rifle, 1× Gauntlets, 1× Sunder Hammer. Gauntlets chip Armor HP (Predictable 1, 5 hits/turn = 5 armor HP/turn). Sunder peels Armor Value (Shred 1). Once armor breaks, rifles finish. ~35% kill in 4 rounds vs Heavy Plate. 100% armor break rate.

Loadout: 2× Corp Rifle, 1× Great Maul, 1× Sunder Greatsword. Maul ignores absorb + direct armor damage. Greatsword shreds value. 99% kill in 4 rounds vs Heavy Plate. Fast but enormous weapon investment.

Loadout: 2× Corp Rifle, 1× Gauntlets + Stiletto (dual wield), 1× Sunder Hammer. The dual wielder chips armor with gauntlets while attempting poison delivery through the Stiletto's Pierce tag. Even 1 damage through gets poison ticking. By round 3: poison stacking, armor cracking, rifles punching through.

Siege Rig (Armor 8/die, Absorb 8, 30 Armor HP) requires dedicated anti-armor from multiple squad members. A single specialist is insufficient. These are boss fights requiring pre-contract preparation.
